course.title: Evidence-Driven Programming Career Launch
course.description: Capstone-ready learners who want a truthful, sustainable, accessible, evidence-driven route from demonstrated computing competence to a well-evaluated programming opportunity and responsible transition. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative career launch from learner constraints and evidence inventory through labor-market research, role decomposition, gap experiments, safe public proof, github and case studies, resumes and application packets, search operations, employer trust, reciprocal relationships, interviews, accommodations, offers, negotiation, transition, and continuing development through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate five authentic career campaign projects, misconception-targeted theory, guided workshops, faded debugging, changed-candidate labs, retrieval, scenario quizzes, and a cumulative unfamiliar defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original evidence manifests that make targeting, truthfulness, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, interview reasoning, decision quality, failure repair, and transfer boundaries mechanically reviewable without automating real hiring actions through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves learner agency, target role, truthfulness, personal contribution, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, jurisdiction, process state, changed-case proof, failure repair, and next-decision boundaries before adding complexity.

Fictional browser cases never contact employers or people, submit applications, access accounts, scrape listings, expose personal information, run learner commands, accept terms, negotiate, or make employment decisions.

Passing work requires role-relevant observable evidence, a changed candidate or opportunity case, contradiction or rejected failure, causal repair, accessible artifact, consent and privacy control, dated source limits, an explicit non-claim, and learner-owned transfer decision.

Employment law, wages, rights, authorization, benefits, tax, contract, background-check, and accommodation details are location- and time-dependent; activities must name the jurisdiction and route consequential questions to current official or qualified local guidance.

competencies.statement: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
misconceptions: A successful search has one universal outcome: the highest available salary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.

competencies.statement: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
misconceptions: Every personal constraint must be explained to an employer to be respected.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.

competencies.statement: Model time, money, energy, care, health, and learning capacity under conservative search scenarios and escalation thresholds.
misconceptions: More applications always compensate for an unsustainable search pace.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model time, money, energy, care, health, and learning capacity under conservative search scenarios and escalation thresholds.

competencies.statement: State ethical, safety, accessibility, privacy, employment-type, travel, location, and schedule boundaries before opportunity pressure appears.
misconceptions: Boundaries can be decided after accepting an offer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State ethical, safety, accessibility, privacy, employment-type, travel, location, and schedule boundaries before opportunity pressure appears.

competencies.statement: Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.
misconceptions: Any rejection proves the candidate chose the wrong career.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a learner-controlled review cadence that adapts tactics from evidence without treating rejection as identity evidence.

competencies.statement: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
misconceptions: Only paid employment produces valid professional evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.

competencies.statement: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.
misconceptions: A polished final artifact proves every skill used to create it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.

competencies.statement: Distinguish personal decisions and work from collaboration, templates, tutorials, automation, generative assistance, and reviewer input.
misconceptions: Disclosure weakens strong work and should be avoided.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish personal decisions and work from collaboration, templates, tutorials, automation, generative assistance, and reviewer input.

competencies.statement: Connect one demonstrated behavior to a changed role context without claiming unobserved expertise.
misconceptions: Using the same tool name makes two work contexts equivalent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect one demonstrated behavior to a changed role context without claiming unobserved expertise.

competencies.statement: Reproduce fragile or stale evidence under a changed requirement and record exact pass, failure, repair, and remaining limit.
misconceptions: Old screenshots remain current evidence if the repository still exists.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reproduce fragile or stale evidence under a changed requirement and record exact pass, failure, repair, and remaining limit.

competencies.statement: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.
misconceptions: The same title names the same work at every organization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.

competencies.statement: Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.
misconceptions: A current webpage necessarily contains current data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.

competencies.statement: Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.
misconceptions: The most-mentioned technology is mandatory for every candidate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.

competencies.statement: Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag.
misconceptions: A national median is an appropriate expected offer for one learner.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag.

competencies.statement: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.
misconceptions: Keeping every possible role open always increases opportunity.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

competencies.statement: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.
misconceptions: Matching nouns matters more than understanding expected work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.

competencies.statement: Classify stated requirements as essential evidence, likely preference, negotiable proxy, process constraint, or unresolved question.
misconceptions: Every bullet is an equally rigid screening threshold.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify stated requirements as essential evidence, likely preference, negotiable proxy, process constraint, or unresolved question.

competencies.statement: Map each priority outcome to direct, adjacent, transferable, missing, or contradictory candidate evidence.
misconceptions: A skill either matches perfectly or does not count.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map each priority outcome to direct, adjacent, transferable, missing, or contradictory candidate evidence.

competencies.statement: Separate observable opportunity evidence from speculative culture, prestige, tone, badge, and keyword interpretations.
misconceptions: An enthusiastic job post reliably predicts daily working conditions.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate observable opportunity evidence from speculative culture, prestige, tone, badge, and keyword interpretations.

competencies.statement: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.
misconceptions: Applying has no cost, so every listing deserves a submission.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make and document a bounded apply, investigate, defer, or decline decision from fit, cost, risk, evidence, and learner priorities.

competencies.statement: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.
misconceptions: Every missing keyword needs a new credential before applying.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.

competencies.statement: Design the smallest authentic task that can reveal or improve one missing behavior under a changed case.
misconceptions: Watching an explanation is enough to close a performance gap.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design the smallest authentic task that can reveal or improve one missing behavior under a changed case.

competencies.statement: Precommit a time, evidence, and opportunity-cost stop rule for readiness work.
misconceptions: Preparation should continue until the candidate feels no uncertainty.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Precommit a time, evidence, and opportunity-cost stop rule for readiness work.

competencies.statement: Describe adjacent experience and an active learning boundary truthfully without apologizing or inflating competence.
misconceptions: Candidates must either claim mastery or highlight deficiency.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Describe adjacent experience and an active learning boundary truthfully without apologizing or inflating competence.

competencies.statement: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interleave bounded applications, feedback signals, retrieval, projects, and plan revision without training on random rejection noise.

competencies.statement: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.

competencies.statement: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
misconceptions: Deleting visible text removes all sensitive history and metadata.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.

competencies.statement: Model impersonation, harassment, discrimination, credential theft, doxxing, scraping, and relationship harm for the learner context.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model impersonation, harassment, discrimination, credential theft, doxxing, scraping, and relationship harm for the learner context.

competencies.statement: Configure appropriate names, emails, account recovery, commit identity, domains, and contact channels with explicit tradeoffs.

competencies.statement: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.
misconceptions: A repository set private after exposure restores confidentiality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run content, history, secret, link, permission, accessibility, and changed-audience checks before and after publication.

competencies.statement: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.
misconceptions: More pinned repositories always create a stronger profile.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.

competencies.statement: Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.
misconceptions: A profile README should repeat a complete chronological resume.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.

competencies.statement: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.
misconceptions: A screenshot and technology badge explain a project sufficiently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.

competencies.statement: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.
misconceptions: Repository community files are decorative for solo work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.

competencies.statement: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.
misconceptions: A passing historical workflow badge proves the project works now.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

competencies.statement: Explain the affected people, decision, evidence, constraints, prior alternative, and personal authority without inventing user research.
misconceptions: A compelling case study needs a dramatic origin story even if none occurred.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain the affected people, decision, evidence, constraints, prior alternative, and personal authority without inventing user research.

competencies.statement: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
misconceptions: Naming a modern stack demonstrates architectural judgment.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.

competencies.statement: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.
misconceptions: Portfolio material should hide failed approaches to appear competent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.

competencies.statement: Connect accessibility, security, privacy, testing, operations, and recovery claims to exact automated and human evidence.
misconceptions: One automated score can establish broad quality conformance.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Connect accessibility, security, privacy, testing, operations, and recovery claims to exact automated and human evidence.

competencies.statement: Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative.
misconceptions: Portfolio accessibility matters only for design roles.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative.

competencies.statement: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
misconceptions: A resume must preserve the full chronology with equal detail.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.

competencies.statement: Write concise action, context, decision, behavior, result, and evidence statements without false causality or invented metrics.
misconceptions: Every bullet needs a large percentage to sound credible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write concise action, context, decision, behavior, result, and evidence statements without false causality or invented metrics.

competencies.statement: Translate projects, education, open source, community work, care, career change, and employment into relevant evidence without hiding provenance.
misconceptions: Unpaid or nontraditional experience belongs nowhere on a technical resume.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate projects, education, open source, community work, care, career change, and employment into relevant evidence without hiding provenance.

competencies.statement: Produce readable semantic source, plain-text, and appropriately generated PDF variants that survive selection, extraction, links, zoom, and reflow checks.
misconceptions: Passing an automated resume scanner guarantees human usability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce readable semantic source, plain-text, and appropriately generated PDF variants that survive selection, extraction, links, zoom, and reflow checks.

competencies.statement: Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering.
misconceptions: Small inconsistencies are harmless because reviewers infer intent.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cross-check every date, role, claim, skill, link, file name, page break, contact route, privacy choice, and changed rendering.

competencies.statement: Select and reorder truthful evidence from the role matrix without keyword stuffing or mirroring unsupported requirements.
misconceptions: Tailoring means copying every phrase from the listing.

competencies.statement: Write a concise evidence bridge between the organization problem, relevant contribution, specific proof, and next conversation.
misconceptions: A cover letter should retell the resume in paragraph form.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write a concise evidence bridge between the organization problem, relevant contribution, specific proof, and next conversation.

competencies.statement: Choose or create a bounded work sample that demonstrates the prioritized behavior without exposing protected material or doing unpaid production work.
misconceptions: Any requested take-home task is safe and proportionate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose or create a bounded work sample that demonstrates the prioritized behavior without exposing protected material or doing unpaid production work.

competencies.statement: Request informed reference consent, provide role context, honor contact preferences, and protect reference information.
misconceptions: Listing a person as a reference is acceptable if they once supervised the candidate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request informed reference consent, provide role context, honor contact preferences, and protect reference information.

competencies.statement: Verify instructions, truthful consistency, file accessibility, links, permissions, privacy, final rendering, receipt, and retained revision.
misconceptions: The application portal confirmation proves every uploaded file was correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify instructions, truthful consistency, file accessibility, links, permissions, privacy, final rendering, receipt, and retained revision.

competencies.statement: Model discovery, research, decision, preparation, submission, follow-up, interview, offer, close, and archive states with next actions.
misconceptions: A list of submitted applications is a complete search system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model discovery, research, decision, preparation, submission, follow-up, interview, offer, close, and archive states with next actions.

competencies.statement: Store only decision-necessary opportunity, contact, consent, artifact revision, deadline, outcome, and learning data with retention rules.
misconceptions: Collecting every detail improves future analysis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Store only decision-necessary opportunity, contact, consent, artifact revision, deadline, outcome, and learning data with retention rules.

competencies.statement: Set a sustainable weekly mix of sourcing, evidence improvement, relationships, applications, practice, recovery, and review.
misconceptions: Application count is the best measure of search quality.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set a sustainable weekly mix of sourcing, evidence improvement, relationships, applications, practice, recovery, and review.

competencies.statement: Change one bounded tactic, define an observable intermediate signal, and review enough comparable cases before inference.
misconceptions: Any response-rate change proves the latest tactic caused it.

competencies.statement: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths.
misconceptions: Search systems need no recovery because the data is temporary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect missed dates, stale listings, broken links, duplicate submissions, lost access, and overload with explicit repair and graceful stop paths.

competencies.statement: Verify employer, domain, recruiter, role, process, and contact through independent official channels before sharing sensitive data or money.

competencies.statement: Recognize urgency, premature offers, payment, check, reshipping, credential, identity, task, impersonation, and off-platform pressure patterns.
misconceptions: A scam is obvious because its writing is always poor.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize urgency, premature offers, payment, check, reshipping, credential, identity, task, impersonation, and off-platform pressure patterns.

competencies.statement: Compare official records, products, customers, funding or revenue claims, leadership, security, labor, news, employee, and counterevidence with source limits.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare official records, products, customers, funding or revenue claims, leadership, security, labor, news, employee, and counterevidence with source limits.

competencies.statement: Investigate management, onboarding, team work, accessibility, inclusion, security, on-call, workload, turnover, measurement, and growth through behavior questions.
misconceptions: Culture labels provide stronger evidence than concrete recent examples.

competencies.statement: Pause, preserve non-sensitive evidence, verify, refuse, report through appropriate channels, secure affected accounts, and document uncertainty after suspicious contact.
misconceptions: Continuing the conversation is necessary to prove fraud.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pause, preserve non-sensitive evidence, verify, refuse, report through appropriate channels, secure affected accounts, and document uncertainty after suspicious contact.

competencies.statement: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.
misconceptions: People without industry contacts have no professional network.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.

competencies.statement: Make a bounded, easy-to-decline request for information, feedback, introduction, or context with purpose and time expectations.
misconceptions: Asking directly for a job is the most efficient first contact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make a bounded, easy-to-decline request for information, feedback, introduction, or context with purpose and time expectations.

competencies.statement: Contribute relevant help, documentation, testing, facilitation, learning notes, or community work within actual competence and norms.

competencies.statement: Record promised action, consented channel, timing, privacy boundary, thanks, and a respectful stop rule.
misconceptions: Persistent follow-up proves motivation.

competencies.statement: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Refuse deceptive familiarity, hidden automation, testimonial fabrication, referral pressure, or contact harvesting.

competencies.statement: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.
misconceptions: The goal of an informational interview is to impress the contact.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.

misconceptions: A prestigious title guarantees applicable advice.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Research public context and explain why this person may have relevant perspective without presuming identity, availability, or representation.

competencies.statement: Plan a short accessible conversation with neutral behavior questions, time consent, recording consent, and an easy stopping path.
misconceptions: Recording is acceptable whenever notes would be inconvenient.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan a short accessible conversation with neutral behavior questions, time consent, recording consent, and an easy stopping path.

competencies.statement: Separate observation, practitioner interpretation, candidate inference, contradiction, and unanswered question across multiple sources.
misconceptions: One experienced person can validate an entire career plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate observation, practitioner interpretation, candidate inference, contradiction, and unanswered question across multiple sources.

competencies.statement: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.
misconceptions: The contact owes continuing mentorship after one conversation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.

competencies.statement: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.
misconceptions: A good introduction is one memorized biography for every audience.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.

competencies.statement: Clarify ambiguous role, level, process, location, schedule, compensation, work sample, and decision criteria before answering.
misconceptions: Candidates must answer every question immediately to appear prepared.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Clarify ambiguous role, level, process, location, schedule, compensation, work sample, and decision criteria before answering.

competencies.statement: Respond to irrelevant, sensitive, or jurisdiction-dependent questions by protecting privacy, redirecting to job function, and seeking qualified local guidance when needed.
misconceptions: Every personal question must be answered to remain in consideration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Respond to irrelevant, sensitive, or jurisdiction-dependent questions by protecting privacy, redirecting to job function, and seeking qualified local guidance when needed.

competencies.statement: Request process details and needed access adjustments with learner-controlled disclosure and location-specific rights awareness.
misconceptions: An accommodation request must include a diagnosis and formal legal vocabulary.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request process details and needed access adjustments with learner-controlled disclosure and location-specific rights awareness.

competencies.statement: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.
misconceptions: A friendly conversation implies advancement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

competencies.statement: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
misconceptions: Beginning to code immediately demonstrates seniority.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.

competencies.statement: Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.
misconceptions: The interviewer can infer reasoning from final code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.

competencies.statement: Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.
misconceptions: The optimal algorithm matters before the contract works.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.

competencies.statement: Use first principles, documentation when allowed, a smaller model, experiment, or explicit partial result when recall fails.
misconceptions: Not remembering a named algorithm means the interview is lost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use first principles, documentation when allowed, a smaller model, experiment, or explicit partial result when recall fails.

competencies.statement: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Elicit stakeholders, workload, quality scenarios, data, trust, failure, cost, operations, and evolution before proposing components.

competencies.statement: Compare feasible alternatives against explicit scenarios, limits, evidence, and revision triggers instead of claiming one best architecture.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare feasible alternatives against explicit scenarios, limits, evidence, and revision triggers instead of claiming one best architecture.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend personal contribution, team decisions, failures, accessibility, security, testing, operations, outcomes, assistance, and non-claims with inspectable evidence.

misconceptions: A polished hypothetical answer is stronger than an imperfect real example.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a specific relevant situation and explain context, responsibility, action, decision, observable result, reflection, and transfer limit.

competencies.statement: Describe disagreement, feedback, error, repair, shared credit, power, and changed behavior without blame or false harmony.
misconceptions: Leadership stories require the candidate to be the sole decision maker.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Describe disagreement, feedback, error, repair, shared credit, power, and changed behavior without blame or false harmony.

competencies.statement: Test stated organizational values with recent behavior, incentives, tradeoffs, failure response, affected people, and employee recourse.
misconceptions: Matching personality with a team is objective evidence of culture fit.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test stated organizational values with recent behavior, incentives, tradeoffs, failure response, affected people, and employee recourse.

competencies.statement: Prepare learner-controlled requests for accessible scheduling, format, environment, communication, assessment, breaks, or assistive technology.
misconceptions: Requesting an adjustment signals inability to do the job.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prepare learner-controlled requests for accessible scheduling, format, environment, communication, assessment, breaks, or assistive technology.

competencies.statement: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.
misconceptions: U.S. employment guidance applies globally and permanently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish general ethical practice from location-specific rights, protected categories, disclosure rules, background checks, and qualified advice.

competencies.statement: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.
misconceptions: A verbal promise is equivalent to an enforceable final agreement.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.

competencies.statement: Compare base, variable pay, equity uncertainty, benefits, leave, hours, commute, equipment, learning, stability, risk, and personal constraints.
misconceptions: The highest base salary is always the best economic offer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare base, variable pay, equity uncertainty, benefits, leave, hours, commute, equipment, learning, stability, risk, and personal constraints.

competencies.statement: Build a source-dated occupation, location, level, and responsibility range while separating public data from this employer decision.
misconceptions: A single online salary number establishes fair compensation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build a source-dated occupation, location, level, and responsibility range while separating public data from this employer decision.

competencies.statement: Make a respectful prioritized counterproposal grounded in role value, evidence, market range, alternatives, and non-salary options.
misconceptions: Negotiation succeeds only if every request is accepted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Make a respectful prioritized counterproposal grounded in role value, evidence, market range, alternatives, and non-salary options.

competencies.statement: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.
misconceptions: Accepting quickly demonstrates commitment and protects the offer.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.

competencies.statement: Close accepted, declined, rejected, withdrawn, and expired opportunities with concise communication, returned property, retained records, and privacy-safe deletion.
misconceptions: Only successful processes deserve professional closure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close accepted, declined, rejected, withdrawn, and expired opportunities with concise communication, returned property, retained records, and privacy-safe deletion.

competencies.statement: Separate direct feedback, process outcome, missing signal, candidate inference, repeated pattern, and uncontrollable variance before changing strategy.
misconceptions: Every rejection contains an actionable verdict about candidate ability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate direct feedback, process outcome, missing signal, candidate inference, repeated pattern, and uncontrollable variance before changing strategy.

competencies.statement: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
misconceptions: Portfolio value justifies retaining prior employer material.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.

competencies.statement: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments.
misconceptions: A new hire should prove value by making major changes immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan accessible onboarding around relationships, product and user context, systems, quality, security, operating norms, early evidence, feedback, and bounded commitments.

competencies.statement: Maintain a consented private evidence log, retrieve skills, seek feedback, update public proof selectively, and review direction without exposing confidential work.
misconceptions: Career development happens only during the next job search.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a consented private evidence log, retrieve skills, seek feedback, update public proof selectively, and review direction without exposing confidential work.

modules.title: Career Agency, Success Criteria, and Search Constraints
modules.description: A graduate is told to apply everywhere immediately, but has not defined acceptable work, financial runway, access needs, location, schedule, authorization, values, safety, or a stop rule. Learners cumulatively produce learner-owned career decision charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a personally meaningful career outcome with observable success, guardrails, tradeoffs, and a review date.
Separate hard constraints, flexible preferences, assumptions, unknowns, and resources without disclosing private reasons unnecessarily.
Model time, money, energy, care, health, and learning capacity under conservative search scenarios and escalation thresholds.

modules.title: Competency Inventory and Independent Evidence Ledger
modules.description: A candidate lists technologies and course completions, yet cannot show the behavior, changed case, personal contribution, limitation, or current reproducibility behind any claim. Learners cumulatively produce competency-to-evidence ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Translate course, work, community, care, creative, and self-directed experience into observable role-relevant behaviors.
Rank claim evidence by authenticity, recency, independence, changed-case performance, stakeholder relevance, and inspectability.
Distinguish personal decisions and work from collaboration, templates, tutorials, automation, generative assistance, and reviewer input.

modules.title: Labor-Market Research, Role Families, and Data Limits
modules.description: A candidate follows viral title and salary lists that mix countries, seniority, occupations, posting windows, and unsupported predictions. Learners cumulatively produce dated labor-market evidence brief through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.
Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.
Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.

modules.title: Job Description Decomposition and Evidence Mapping
modules.description: A listing combines recruiter language, team outcomes, essential work, preferences, copied requirements, benefits, legal text, and ambiguous signals. Learners cumulatively produce role requirement and evidence matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Extract the problems, users, decisions, deliverables, collaboration, quality, and operating outcomes beneath requirement wording.
Classify stated requirements as essential evidence, likely preference, negotiable proxy, process constraint, or unresolved question.
Map each priority outcome to direct, adjacent, transferable, missing, or contradictory candidate evidence.

modules.title: Gap Closure, Deliberate Practice, and Honest Positioning
modules.description: The role matrix exposes a few high-value gaps, but the candidate starts another broad course instead of testing whether each gap blocks real work. Learners cumulatively produce time-boxed role-readiness experiment plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Prioritize gaps by role frequency, task centrality, transfer distance, evidence weakness, learning cost, and application timing.
Design the smallest authentic task that can reveal or improve one missing behavior under a changed case.
Precommit a time, evidence, and opportunity-cost stop rule for readiness work.

modules.title: Public Identity, Privacy, Security, and Reputation Boundaries
modules.description: A learner is told to make everything public, revealing contact details, location patterns, secrets, client material, protected identity, private history, and unsafe availability. Learners cumulatively produce public-identity threat and disclosure model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose a public, selective, private, or live-only evidence channel from audience need, learner safety, and claim purpose.
Inventory personal, third-party, employer, client, research, secret, metadata, and inferred information before publication.
Model impersonation, harassment, discrimination, credential theft, doxxing, scraping, and relationship harm for the learner context.

modules.title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work
modules.description: A profile has a dense contribution graph and many forked tutorials, but reviewers cannot find three relevant, runnable, maintained, or honestly attributed examples. Learners cumulatively produce selective GitHub evidence route through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select a small set of projects by target-role relevance, independent contribution, evidence strength, recency, and safe disclosure.
Write a concise profile route that helps a reviewer reach role, proof, boundaries, contact choice, and accessible alternatives.
Create a fast repository entry path through purpose, users, status, demonstration, setup, test, architecture, and limitation evidence.

modules.title: Portfolio Case Studies and Technical Decision Narratives
modules.description: A project page celebrates features and tools but omits the user need, personal decisions, alternatives, failures, verification, accessibility, security, impact limits, and maintenance state. Learners cumulatively produce evidence-linked project case-study set through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain the affected people, decision, evidence, constraints, prior alternative, and personal authority without inventing user research.
Present important alternatives, tradeoffs, revision triggers, and the learner-owned technical decisions rather than a tool inventory.
Show a reproduced failure, competing hypotheses, causal repair, regression evidence, and what the repair did not prove.

modules.title: Resume Architecture, Evidence Statements, and Machine-Robust Delivery
modules.description: A resume uses decorative columns, keyword blocks, vague adjectives, unsupported impact numbers, and identical content for every role. Learners cumulatively produce truthful role-targeted resume system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
Write concise action, context, decision, behavior, result, and evidence statements without false causality or invented metrics.
Translate projects, education, open source, community work, care, career change, and employment into relevant evidence without hiding provenance.

modules.title: Tailored Applications, Work Samples, and References
modules.description: A candidate replaces terms mechanically, writes a generic cover letter, uploads arbitrary work, and shares reference contact details without permission. Learners cumulatively produce auditable role-specific application packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Select and reorder truthful evidence from the role matrix without keyword stuffing or mirroring unsupported requirements.
Write a concise evidence bridge between the organization problem, relevant contribution, specific proof, and next conversation.
Choose or create a bounded work sample that demonstrates the prioritized behavior without exposing protected material or doing unpaid production work.

modules.title: Job Search Operating System, Experiments, and Sustainable Throughput
modules.description: A candidate relies on browser tabs and memory, duplicates work, misses follow-ups, exposes sensitive notes, and changes tactics after every outcome. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-preserving search decision system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model discovery, research, decision, preparation, submission, follow-up, interview, offer, close, and archive states with next actions.
Store only decision-necessary opportunity, contact, consent, artifact revision, deadline, outcome, and learning data with retention rules.
Set a sustainable weekly mix of sourcing, evidence improvement, relationships, applications, practice, recovery, and review.

modules.title: Employer Research, Opportunity Quality, and Scam Defense
modules.description: An urgent remote offer uses a recognizable logo, personal email, text interview, early identity request, equipment check, and no independently verifiable team. Learners cumulatively produce opportunity trust and risk dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Verify employer, domain, recruiter, role, process, and contact through independent official channels before sharing sensitive data or money.
Recognize urgency, premature offers, payment, check, reshipping, credential, identity, task, impersonation, and off-platform pressure patterns.
Compare official records, products, customers, funding or revenue claims, leadership, security, labor, news, employee, and counterevidence with source limits.

modules.title: Professional Relationships, Community Participation, and Consent
modules.description: A learner treats networking as mass cold requests for referrals, extracts attention, ignores community norms, and measures people only by hiring power. Learners cumulatively produce reciprocal relationship practice plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map existing reciprocal relationships across work, study, community, identity-safe spaces, events, and weak ties without ranking human worth.
Make a bounded, easy-to-decline request for information, feedback, introduction, or context with purpose and time expectations.
Contribute relevant help, documentation, testing, facilitation, learning notes, or community work within actual competence and norms.

modules.title: Informational Interviews and Reality Testing
modules.description: A candidate schedules a conversation to ask for a hidden referral, asks generic questions available online, records without consent, and treats one opinion as market truth. Learners cumulatively produce consented role-reality interview study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.
Research public context and explain why this person may have relevant perspective without presuming identity, availability, or representation.
Plan a short accessible conversation with neutral behavior questions, time consent, recording consent, and an easy stopping path.

modules.title: Recruiter Screens, Written Communication, and Process Control
modules.description: A fast screening call mixes role interest, evidence, compensation, location, authorization, accommodations, private history, and unclear next steps. Learners cumulatively produce screening conversation and process record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.
Clarify ambiguous role, level, process, location, schedule, compensation, work sample, and decision criteria before answering.
Respond to irrelevant, sensitive, or jurisdiction-dependent questions by protecting privacy, redirecting to job function, and seeking qualified local guidance when needed.

modules.title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries
modules.description: A timed exercise rewards silent pattern recall while the candidate ignores requirements, changed cases, testing, communication, accessibility, security, tool policy, and task proportionality. Learners cumulatively produce observable technical interview practice system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
Externalize assumptions, alternatives, invariants, tradeoffs, checkpoints, uncertainty, and help requests without narrating every keystroke.
Build the smallest correct behavior, test examples and changed cases, inspect failures, then improve measured cost or design.

modules.title: Pairing, System Reasoning, Debugging, and Project Defense
modules.description: A candidate treats collaboration as performance theater, accepts false premises, over-designs an abstract system, and describes a team project as entirely personal work. Learners cumulatively produce multi-format technical defense packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Establish roles, turn-taking, accessibility, tool use, communication, disagreement, and shared success for a collaborative exercise.
Reproduce the symptom, bound the last-known-good state, rank hypotheses, run discriminating checks, repair the cause, and retain regression evidence.
Elicit stakeholders, workload, quality scenarios, data, trust, failure, cost, operations, and evolution before proposing components.

modules.title: Behavioral Evidence, Values, Equity, and Accessible Hiring
modules.description: A candidate memorizes heroic stories, erases collaborators, blames others, performs culture fit, and assumes every hiring task is equally accessible or lawful everywhere. Learners cumulatively produce behavioral evidence and access response bank through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose a specific relevant situation and explain context, responsibility, action, decision, observable result, reflection, and transfer limit.
Describe disagreement, feedback, error, repair, shared credit, power, and changed behavior without blame or false harmony.
Test stated organizational values with recent behavior, incentives, tradeoffs, failure response, affected people, and employee recourse.

modules.title: Offer Evaluation, Compensation, Negotiation, and Decision Quality
modules.description: A verbal offer creates urgency while base pay, equity, benefits, schedule, location, probation, intellectual property, contingencies, and role expectations remain unclear. Learners cumulatively produce evidence-based offer decision model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.
Compare base, variable pay, equity uncertainty, benefits, leave, hours, commute, equipment, learning, stability, risk, and personal constraints.
Build a source-dated occupation, location, level, and responsibility range while separating public data from this employer decision.

modules.title: Ethical Transition, Rejection Learning, and Continuing Career Development
modules.description: After an outcome, the candidate either stops learning, extracts certainty from sparse feedback, burns relationships, or carries confidential material into the next role. Learners cumulatively produce first-90-days and continuing career evidence plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Close accepted, declined, rejected, withdrawn, and expired opportunities with concise communication, returned property, retained records, and privacy-safe deletion.
Separate direct feedback, process outcome, missing signal, candidate inference, repeated pattern, and uncontrollable variance before changing strategy.
Honor notice, confidentiality, intellectual property, client, account, device, access, data, and relationship duties with qualified local review where needed.
